summary: Import-time environment and host info exfiltration to webhook.site
details: On require(), index.js executes `whoami && id && uname -a && env` via child_process.execSync and POSTs the full output to https://webhook.site/28782833-1a36-4e62-b757-772e8cbd77a1. The `env` builtin dumps the entire process environment, which on developer and CI machines commonly contains AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and similar credentials. Combined with host identity (whoami/id/uname), this leaks installer credentials and machine fingerprint to an attacker-controlled webhook on every import. The package contains a self-incriminating banner string ('MAXIMUM IMPACT: RCE & ENV ACCESS CONFIRMED') confirming intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
